channel.title: Data-Engineering on Lough on Data
channel.description: Recent content in Data-Engineering on Lough on Data

item.title: Databox
item.author: connor@loughondata.com (Connor Lough)
item.description: A single-operator data platform that ingests eBird, NOAA, and USGS into one queryable warehouse — built to answer whether species distributions shift with same-day weather and streamflow anomalies.

item.title: Never Make Another dbt model .yml
item.author: connor@loughondata.com (Connor Lough)
item.description: A GitHub Action that runs dbt-osmosis + dbt2looker on every push, then commits the regenerated .yml and .lookml files back to your repo.

item.title: dlt Demo: Bird Hotspot Finder, pt. II
item.author: connor@loughondata.com (Connor Lough)
item.description: Extending the dlt pipeline: stage load packages to S3, persist schema changes back to DuckDB, and ping Slack on schema drift.

item.title: dlt Demo: Bird Hotspot Finder, pt. I
item.author: connor@loughondata.com (Connor Lough)
item.description: Loading eBird API data into DuckDB with dlt, then querying it in DBeaver. A first look at dlt as a Meltano/Airbyte alternative.

item.title: Creating a Mermaid Diagram for Your dbt Project Using graph.gpickle
item.author: connor@loughondata.com (Connor Lough)
item.description: Turn a dbt project’s compiled graph.gpickle into a Mermaid diagram so you can actually see what your models depend on.

item.title: Creating a Data Pipeline Between PostgreSQL and Snowflake with Meltano
item.author: connor@loughondata.com (Connor Lough)
item.description: Standing up a Meltano pipeline from a PostgreSQL source to a Snowflake destination — init, plugins, env config, run.
